author: Claude
description: Returns sales/transaction data grouped by 4 day-part time buckets across a date range and sites with YoY comparison. Multi-site support via Expand Inline. Single-scan optimization for ~0.6s performance.

parameters.name: @SiteIds
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of Site IDs. ⚠️ CRITICAL: Set Expand Inline = YES in OutSystems!

parameters.name: @SelectedView
parameters.description: Metric to display: D=Dollar Sales, G=Guest Count, A=Average Check

output_columns.name: DayPartLabel
output_columns.description: Day part name (Total, Overnight, Breakfast, Day, Night)

output_columns.name: PercentTotal
output_columns.description: Percentage of that site's daily total (0 for Average view)

tables_used.purpose: Sales transaction data with DateTime and metrics
Site master data (joined on Site.Id = SalesFact.SiteId)

key_features: ⚠️ Expand Inline = YES for @SiteIds (no SQL parsing needed!)
🔥 Single-scan optimization (reads SalesFact ONCE for CY+PY)
Pre-calculated NZ timezone conversion (expensive op done once)
YearType flag + conditional aggregation for CY/PY pivot
Multi-site support via comma-separated Site IDs
Tenant filtering handled by OutSystems application layer
4 day-part time buckets (Overnight, Breakfast, Day, Night)
Window functions for % calculations (partitioned by site)
Numbers CTE for large date ranges

techniques: Expand Inline = YES (no SQL parsing needed)
Single-scan for CY+PY data (was 2 separate scans)

related_queries.name: Product Sales By Day Part Hourly
Product Sales By POS Type Hourly

output_structure.sort_order: Date ASC, SiteName ASC, SortOrder ASC

change_log.change: Initial creation - parent query setup from user-provided code
change_log.author: Claude

change_log.change: Added multi-site support - @SiteId nullable, @ActiveOnly parameter, Site table join, SiteFilter CTE
change_log.author: Claude

change_log.change: REFACTORED to @SiteIds approach - comma-separated list, tenant filtering moved to OutSystems
change_log.author: Claude

change_log.change: MAJOR OPTIMIZATION: (1) Expand Inline = YES for SiteIds (no SQL parsing needed), (2) Single-scan for CY+PY data (was 2 scans), (3) Pre-calculated NZ timezone before aggregation, (4) YearType flag + conditional aggregation. Target: ~0.6s performance.
change_log.author: Claude
